revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Princess Naniwa no Ono]][[fa:شاهدخت نان‌ایوا نو اونو]][[ja:難波小野王]][[zh:难波小野王]] {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|難波小野王|date=January 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Naniwa no Ono |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 485–489 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = Prince Oka-no-Wakugo |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = 489 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Naniwa no Ono''' (? – 489) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzō]].<ref>Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>
Princess Naniwa was the daughter of Prince Oka-no-Wakugo and great-granddaughter of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]].
Naniwa committed suicide due to fears over disrespecting [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] when he was crown prince.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] | years=485–489}} {{s-end}}
{{Consorts of Japan}}
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
[[en:Princess Naniwa no Ono]]
[[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q11659541}}

{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Naniwa no Ono |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 485–489 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = Prince Oka-no-Wakugo |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = 489 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Naniwa no Ono''' (? – 489) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzō]].<ref>Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>
Princess Naniwa was the daughter of Prince Oka-no-Wakugo and great-granddaughter of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]].
Naniwa committed suicide due to fears over disrespecting [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] when he was crown prince.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] | years=485–489}} {{s-end}}
{{Consorts of Japan}}

'''Princess Naniwa no Ono''' (? – 489) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzō}}.<ref>Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>

Princess Naniwa was the daughter of Prince Oka-no-Wakugo and great-granddaughter of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}.

Naniwa committed suicide due to fears over disrespecting {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} when he was crown prince.
